commands.repair_reason: - [P1] Human review is needed because this security-scan trust-boundary PR has a concrete validator blocker and lacks contributor real worker proof.; Needs attention: The patch improves the scan trust boundary but needs a validator fix before it reliably enforces its own coverage-proof contract.; Review finding: [P2] Reject unread coverage before accepting ranges — scripts/security/run-codex-scan-worker.ts:1029-1031 (sha=5a033d9ca3bc1c45a247c76761eb5fb5d6247db6)
commands.review_summary:
This PR adds `artifact_coverage` to the Codex scan output schema and prompt, rejects benign worker results without submitted-file coverage proof, adds worker tests, and documents the invariant in `specs/security-moderation.md`.

Reproducibility: yes. Source inspection shows a benign result with `coverage: "metadata_only"` or `"not_inspected"` can pass if `ranges` includes `full` or head/tail, because the validator treats ranges as sufficient at the new fallback checks.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Human review is needed because this security-scan trust-boundary PR has a concrete validator blocker and lacks contributor real worker proof.

target.title: Require Codex scan artifact coverage proof
target.body:
## Summary

- require `artifact_coverage` in the Codex scan worker output schema
- update the worker prompt to make clean verdicts depend on submitted-file coverage, including full coverage for small files and head/tail coverage for large files
- reject benign Codex results before storage when coverage proof is missing, incomplete, or omits tail coverage for large files
- document the worker-time coverage invariant in `specs/security-moderation.md`

## By design

This stays at the worker/schema contract layer because the bypass class is about evaluator coverage: a clean verdict should prove what the evaluator inspected before ClawHub stores it as `clean`. Persisting coverage metadata into Convex/public scan state is not needed for the trust decision yet and would widen the product/API surface. Redacted worker diagnostics still retain the raw Codex result, including coverage proof, for operator debugging.

The validator is intentionally scoped to benign verdicts. Suspicious or malicious outcomes may still be useful when coverage is incomplete, but incomplete coverage can no longer become a stored clean result.

## Validation

- `bun run test scripts/security/run-codex-scan-worker.test.ts`
- `bun run test scripts/security/run-codex-scan-worker.test.ts convex/securityScan.test.ts convex/lib/securityPrompt.test.ts`
- `bunx tsc --noEmit --pretty false`
- `bun run ci:static`

Not run: `autoreview` / Codex review, per delegation.
